name: Altona North
blurb: An affordable, fast-improving family pocket between the city and the bay. Big blocks, new development around the Circle precinct, and strong buyer demand from priced-out inner-west families.
lifestyle: Parklands, the new Altona North town centre, easy West Gate access.
transport: Buses to Newport & Yarraville stations; ~14km to the CBD by car.

name: South Kingsville
blurb: A small, tightly-held enclave of period homes with a genuine village feel. Rarely-traded streets and a loyal local community keep competition high.
lifestyle: The South Kingsville shops, Donald McLean Reserve, cafe culture.
transport: Walk to Spotswood station; quick run to the West Gate.

name: Newport
blurb: One of the inner west's most liveable suburbs — period homes, Newport Lakes, and a thriving cafe scene. Consistently strong family and downsizer demand.
lifestyle: Newport Lakes Reserve, Hall Street village, the Substation arts hub.
transport: Newport station on the Williamstown & Werribee lines; ~10km to the CBD.

name: Yarraville
blurb: The poster child of the inner west — the iconic Sun Theatre, leafy streets and a buzzing village. Premium prices for renovated period homes near the strip.
lifestyle: Yarraville Village, the Sun Theatre, weekend farmers' markets.

name: Spotswood
blurb: A compact riverside suburb with Scienceworks, growing dining options and quick city access. Increasingly popular with young families and professionals.
lifestyle: Scienceworks, the Hobsons Bay Coastal Trail, riverside walks.
transport: Spotswood station; minutes to the West Gate Bridge.

name: Williamstown
blurb: The bayside jewel of the west — historic streetscapes, a working harbour and beachfront lifestyle. The premium end of the Manifest service area.
lifestyle: The Strand, Williamstown Beach, Nelson Place dining, the ferry to the city.
transport: Williamstown station; seasonal ferry to Docklands.

name: Seddon
blurb: A village-scale suburb wedged between Footscray and Yarraville, beloved for its Victorian cottages and its tight, walkable shopping strip.
lifestyle: Charles Street village, Bibby Street cafes, easy reach of Footscray Market.

name: Keilor East
blurb: An established middle-ring suburb where mid-century family homes are increasingly replaced by quality double-storey townhouses. Strong appeal to families wanting space without inner-city prices.
lifestyle: Milleara Mall, the Maribyrnong River parklands, Keilor village dining.
transport: Buses to Keilor Plains & Niddrie; quick CityLink/Tullamarine Fwy access, ~13km to the CBD.

name: Sunbury
blurb: A semi-rural township on the city's north-west edge, growing fast as buyers chase larger blocks and value. Big house-and-land estates sit alongside an established town centre.
lifestyle: Sunbury town centre, Jacksons Creek trails, wineries and the Macedon Ranges nearby.
transport: Sunbury station (end of the metro line); ~20 min to Melbourne Airport, ~40 min to the CBD.

name: Beveridge
blurb: One of Melbourne's fastest-growing northern frontier suburbs, anchored by the approved Beveridge Central PSP. Almost entirely new house-and-land and development land, with major price growth as infrastructure lands.
lifestyle: New masterplanned estates (Lyra, Mandalay), parks and schools rolling out; country outlook.
transport: Hume Freeway on the doorstep; future Beveridge station planned; ~45km to the CBD.

name: Wallan
blurb: A growing northern township around the Hidden Valley golf community, popular with families and tree-changers after acreage and lifestyle blocks at accessible prices.
lifestyle: Hidden Valley Golf & Country Club, Wallan town centre, Mount Disappointment forest.
transport: Wallan station on the Seymour line; Hume Freeway access, ~50km to the CBD.

name: Werribee
blurb: A major established outer-west centre with its own hospital, university and town heart, surrounded by fast-growing new estates. Deep, value-driven buyer demand from first-home owners and investors.
lifestyle: Werribee Open Range Zoo, the Mansion & State Rose Garden, Werribee Plaza, riverside parks.
transport: Werribee station on the metro line; Princes Freeway access, ~32km to the CBD.

name: Weir Views
blurb: A newer estate suburb beside Melton South, made up almost entirely of modern family homes. Affordable, well-serviced and popular with growing families.
lifestyle: Lake-side reserves, new schools and shopping, the Toolern development precinct.
transport: Close to Melton & Cobblebank stations; Western Freeway access, ~40km to the CBD.

name: Winter Valley
blurb: A rapidly expanding estate suburb on Ballarat's western edge within the approved Ballarat West PSP. New homes and large lots at some of the most accessible price points in the region.
lifestyle: Aberdeen Estate parklands, new schools, minutes to Ballarat's amenities and the Goldfields.
transport: Near Wendouree & Ballarat stations; Western Freeway access, ~110km to the CBD.
